reasoning: The recall was issued on March 28, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025, providing a window of approximately 95 days. Based on FSIS procedures (Directive 8080.1), a recall is only closed after the company has completed all recovery actions and the FSIS has verified the effectiveness of the recall. This process, including the submission of final reports and administrative review, typically takes significantly longer than three months, even for Class III recalls which are lower risk. Crucially, evidence indicates that some of the recalled liquid egg products have expiration dates as late as August 9, 2025 (search_3_result_8). FSIS is unlikely to close a recall while potentially contaminated product remains within its shelf life and could still be in consumer refrigerators. The combination of a nationwide distribution, a tight 95-day window, and product shelf life extending past the deadline makes an early closure highly improbable.
